title: Corporate Immigration Program Development | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Corporate Immigration Program Development

### Optimize your immigration operations.
Maybe you’re a start-up, getting ready to hire an employee from a foreign country for the first time. Maybe you’re a burgeoning small or medium-sized enterprise, ramping up your international operations. Or maybe you’re part of a busy global mobility team within a large multinational company trying to find more efficiencies or reduce costs.
Regardless of your current immigration situation, an effective immigration policy and program is essential to meeting your immediate business needs and long-term goals.
As a firm solely dedicated to immigration, we’ve seen—and built—a lot of different corporate immigration programs over the years. We know what works best for companies like yours and can provide targeted guidance on immigration program design, management and best practices to help you to optimize your immigration operations.

### Strategic, world-class corporate immigration policy and program development.
Fragomen partners with companies of all sizes to develop customized immigration programs and policies in line with unique corporate preferences and practices—while taking advantage of processing and cost efficiencies.
Our corporate immigration policy services include:
- Immigration policy and program design assistance
- Reviewing and revising existing policies and procedures
- Strategic planning and comprehensive local, regional or global program management, utilizing:
- Case management technology Reporting capabilities Performance metrics Industry benchmarking for best practices
- Case management technology Reporting capabilities Performance metrics Industry benchmarking for best practices
- Case management technology
- Reporting capabilities
- Industry benchmarking for best practices
- Workflow analyses to achieve maximum efficiency in case preparation and data-sharing
- Due diligence guidance regarding the impact of immigration regulations and requirements on corporate restructuring, including:
- Mergers and acquisitions Divestitures Reductions in force
- Mergers and acquisitions Divestitures Reductions in force
- Mergers and acquisitions
- Short-term assignment planning and back-to-back assignment strategies
Integrated Mobility Programs
Fragomen strives to facilitate simplified and efficient mobility processes. We can work with you and your other mobility vendors, such as your relocation and tax services providers, to develop integrated mobility programs and solutions. This helps to achieve synergies, efficiencies and a reduction in the number of touchpoints across the mobility ecosystem.
We focus on creating streamlined integration for our clients and we have built processes and standards that facilitate information exchange and promote data integrity.

### Evolving your policy and program with the future.
Fragomen will partner with you every step of the way as you grow and evolve. We can help you proactively adjust your immigration policies and program where needed at different stages of growth or when regulations change, and we will introduce new opportunities and technologies to help support you as you continue to expand long term.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
